title: Account id in Webhook
excerpt:
Hi, 
I am working on a monday app integration,where a column change triggers an event and that event is sent to web url specified. I am receiving the event to url I have specified. Is there any way to identify from which…

posts.author: chandan (chandan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
I am working on a monday app integration,where a column change triggers an event and that event is sent to web url specified. I am receiving the event to url I have specified. Is there any way to identify from which account(account id) this event has been triggered from?

I need this information as I have a custom requirement to identify which application has sent this event

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: The accounId is not part of the event. In my use cases it is sufficient to know that the boardId is unique, so I don’t need the accountId. If you really need to know the only solution I see is to store the accounId in a database table whit an unique key boardId.

posts.author: chandan (chandan)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the reply, is boardId unique across accounts?. And also I have one more question, can the same user(with same user id) be part of multiple accounts?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, boardId is unique across all monday accounts (that is what monday told me ). The same user (email, name etc) can exist in multiple accounts but the user gets a different userId in each account (maybe worht double checking with support).

posts.author: dsilva (Daniel Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @chandan - apologies for the delay here.

As Bas mentioned above, the boardID is unique across the platform.

Let me know if there’s anything else I can help with!

-Daniel

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
